title: Concurrent Cache Access, Duplicate Suppression, Ownership, and Race Evidence · Go Build Room
summary: A regional field-research team incrementally builds race-free duplicate-suppressed resource loader from original fixed PokéAPI fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP, Git, quality-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of race-free duplicate-suppressed resource loader while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugg…
objectives: Protect shared cache invariants with a mutex or owned goroutine and keep lock scope away from slow network work.
Represent one canonical in-flight fetch, waiters, completion, result, and cleanup without leaking entries.
Let each waiter cancel independently while defining when shared underlying work should continue or stop.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pokedex Go Cache Synchron…
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 8726 during a narrow-terminal ranger workflow. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove duplicate suppression, independent waiters, safe publication, and in-flight cleanup; the release claim must identify the exact source and artifact revision; then inject one cache, waiter, goroutine, cancellation, or queue schedule and retain ownership evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: pokedex-go-concurrency-test-evidence-1serc4" that uses case 4273 to prove duplicate suppression, independent waiters, safe publication, and in-flight cleanup. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact PokéAPI, read or write host files, handle real terminal or OS signals, spawn unbounded work, or claim race, leak, load, accessibility, cross-platform, package, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, race and fuzz runs, temporary files, subprocess and signal harnesses, inspected artifacts, representative platforms, accessibility review, capacity, rollback, recovery, and release gates.
